weight_scale.description: Multiplicative amplification factor. 1.0 = source domain progress has a measured but modest effect. 2.0 = source demonstrably accelerates target by roughly an order of magnitude on key metrics. 3.0 = source is the primary driver of target domain progress.

changelog_from_v5.ai_to_ai: Reduced from 2.5 to 1.8. See detailed justification.
changelog_from_v5.ai_to_drug: Reduced from 3.0 to 2.2. Single demonstration, not yet replicated at scale.
changelog_from_v5.ai_to_materials: Reduced from 3.0 to 2.5. GNoME is transformative but most discoveries not yet synthesized.
changelog_from_v5.ai_to_robotics: Reduced from 2.5 to 2.0. Foundation models impressive but deployment narrow.

interactions.weight_justification: This is the most consequential parameter in the model and deserves the most scrutiny. The v5 weight of 2.5 was too high because it conflated demonstrated narrow improvements with general recursive self-improvement. Evidence supports three channels: (1) NAS reduced architecture search time ~100x but only within constrained search spaces (Zoph & Le 2017, confirmed by EfficientNet 2019); (2) AI-designed chip layouts show 10-30% PPA improvement per generation (AlphaChip/Nature 2024), which is meaningful but comparable to expert human iteration; (3) AI coding assistants show 2-4x productivity on isolated tasks (GitHub 2022 study: 55% faster on specific tasks) but much less on complex system design. Critically, none of these demonstrate true recursive improvement where AI capability compounds on itself without human guidance. The strongest evidence for recursive improvement is in narrow domains (NAS improving NAS), not general capability. Weight reduced to 1.8 to reflect documented narrow improvements without assuming general recursive acceleration that has not been demonstrated.

evidence.description: Neural Architecture Search (NAS) reduced architecture design time from months to hours, but within constrained search spaces
evidence.measured_amplification: ~100x reduction in architecture search time for specific model families
evidence.source: Zoph & Le, 'Neural Architecture Search with Reinforcement Learning', ICLR 2017; confirmed by Tan & Le, 'EfficientNet', ICML 2019
evidence.caveat: Search space must be defined by humans. NAS finds optimal configurations within human-designed constraints, not fundamentally new paradigms.

evidence.description: AlphaChip (Google): AI-designed chip layouts matched or exceeded human expert designs for TPU blocks
evidence.measured_amplification: 10-30% improvement in power-performance-area (PPA) metrics; design time reduced from weeks to hours
evidence.source: Mirhoseini et al., 'A graph placement methodology for fast chip design', Nature 2021; updated results in Nature 2024
evidence.caveat: Applicable to placement/routing, not full chip architecture. Human architects still design the overall chip. Improvement magnitude comparable to one generation of human iteration.

evidence.description: GitHub Copilot controlled study: 55% faster task completion for AI-assisted developers
evidence.measured_amplification: 1.55x speed on isolated coding tasks; 2-4x reported in some follow-up studies for boilerplate code
evidence.source: Peng et al., 'The Impact of AI on Developer Productivity: Evidence from GitHub Copilot', arXiv 2023
evidence.caveat: Measured on isolated tasks, not full software projects. Complex system design, debugging, and architecture decisions show much smaller gains. Google internal study (2024) found ~30% productivity gain on average across real projects.

evidence.description: AI-generated training data and synthetic data augmentation improving model performance
evidence.measured_amplification: 5-20% accuracy improvements on benchmarks when using AI-generated training data
evidence.source: Multiple papers including Gunasekar et al., 'Textbooks Are All You Need', 2023
evidence.caveat: Model collapse risk when training on AI-generated data (Shumailov et al., Nature 2024). Not a pure positive feedback loop.

counter_evidence.description: Model collapse: training AI on AI-generated data leads to quality degradation over generations
counter_evidence.source: Shumailov et al., 'AI models collapse when trained on recursively generated data', Nature 2024

counter_evidence.description: Scaling laws show diminishing returns: each 10x compute increase yields roughly linear benchmark improvement, not exponential
counter_evidence.source: Epoch AI, 'Scaling Laws Literature Review', 2024

counter_evidence.description: No demonstrated case of AI recursively improving its own general capability without human researchers guiding the process
counter_evidence.source: Bostrom & Shulman, 'Sharing the World with Digital Minds', 2024

saturation.notes: Recursive self-improvement has hard limits: model collapse, diminishing returns from scaling laws, and the need for real-world validation. Asymptotic model captures the ceiling.

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged from v5. AI chip design tools (AlphaChip, Synopsys DSO.ai) demonstrably reduce design cycle time. Compiler optimizations via ML (XLA, TVM) yield 10-40% throughput improvements. Well-documented with multiple independent confirmations.

evidence.description: AlphaChip reduced TPU chip layout design from weeks to hours with comparable or better PPA
evidence.measured_amplification: Design cycle: weeks to hours (~50-100x time reduction). PPA: 10-30% improvement.
evidence.source: Mirhoseini et al., Nature 2021/2024; Google DeepMind
evidence.caveat: Placement and routing only, not full architecture design

evidence.description: Synopsys DSO.ai: commercial AI-driven chip design achieving 20% PPA improvement
evidence.measured_amplification: ~20% PPA improvement, 3-5x reduction in design iterations
evidence.source: Synopsys DSO.ai product documentation and customer case studies, 2023
evidence.caveat: Commercial claims, though confirmed by multiple semiconductor customers including Samsung

evidence.description: ML compiler optimizations (XLA, TVM, Halide) yield 10-40% throughput improvement over hand-tuned code
evidence.measured_amplification: 10-40% computational throughput improvement
evidence.source: Chen et al., 'TVM: An Automated End-to-End Optimizing Compiler', OSDI 2018; Google XLA documentation
evidence.caveat: Improvements are one-time per hardware generation, not compounding

counter_evidence.description: Moore's Law slowdown is driven by physics (lithography limits), not design — AI cannot overcome atomic-scale constraints
counter_evidence.source: IEEE International Roadmap for Devices and Systems (IRDS), 2024

saturation.notes: Design optimization has diminishing returns as designs approach physical limits

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. DeepMind's 40% data centre cooling reduction is well-documented but narrow. AI grid optimization shows 5-15% efficiency gains. Solid but not transformative — energy costs are dominated by hardware and physics, not optimization.

evidence.description: DeepMind AI reduced Google data centre cooling energy by 40%
evidence.measured_amplification: 40% reduction in cooling energy (which is ~30% of total data centre energy), net ~12% total energy reduction
evidence.source: DeepMind Blog, 'DeepMind AI Reduces Google Data Centre Cooling Bill by 40%', 2016
evidence.caveat: Cooling is only part of data centre energy. Total energy reduction is ~12%, not 40%. Also, increased AI compute demand may offset savings.

evidence.description: AI-driven grid optimization: 5-15% efficiency improvement in power distribution
evidence.measured_amplification: 5-15% reduction in transmission losses and peak demand
evidence.source: National Renewable Energy Laboratory (NREL), 'AI for Grid Modernization', 2023
evidence.caveat: Grid improvements are incremental and constrained by physical infrastructure

counter_evidence.description: AI training and inference are themselves massive energy consumers — net effect on energy system may be negative
counter_evidence.source: IEA, 'Electricity 2024: Analysis and forecast to 2026'

saturation.notes: Optimization gains are bounded by thermodynamic limits

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. Microsoft/PNNL AI-accelerated electrolyte discovery (2024) is the strongest evidence. AI reduced materials screening from years to months. Multiple independent confirmations from battery research labs.

evidence.description: Microsoft and PNNL used AI to identify a new solid-state electrolyte (N2116) in months instead of years
evidence.measured_amplification: Screening time: years to months (~10-50x). Identified viable candidate from 32 million possibilities.
evidence.source: Zuber et al., Microsoft Research / PNNL, 2024
evidence.caveat: Discovery-to-production pipeline still requires years of engineering. The AI accelerated screening, not manufacturing.

evidence.description: Google DeepMind GNoME identified 380,000 stable crystal structures, many relevant to battery chemistry
evidence.measured_amplification: 10x increase in known stable materials, subset relevant to energy storage
evidence.source: Merchant et al., 'Scaling deep learning for materials discovery', Nature 2023
evidence.caveat: Predicted stability, not all experimentally verified. Fraction relevant to batteries is small.

counter_evidence.description: Battery cost declines are primarily driven by manufacturing scale (Wright's Law), not materials discovery
counter_evidence.source: BloombergNEF Battery Price Survey 2024

saturation.notes: Materials discovery accelerates early-stage R&D but manufacturing learning curves dominate cost

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. AlphaFold is one of the most significant AI applications to date. popEVE for variant classification is transformative. Multiple well-documented applications across genomics.

evidence.description: AlphaFold2 solved the protein structure prediction problem, predicting structures for 200M+ proteins
evidence.measured_amplification: Accuracy matching experimental methods (GDT >90). Reduced structure determination from months/years to seconds.
evidence.source: Jumper et al., 'Highly accurate protein structure prediction with AlphaFold', Nature 2021
evidence.caveat: Predicts static structures; protein dynamics and interactions less well-captured. AlphaFold3 extends to complexes but with limitations.

evidence.description: popEVE: AI model classifying 100+ million disease-associated genetic variants
evidence.measured_amplification: Classified ~100M variants with clinical-grade accuracy for many genes
evidence.source: Frazer et al., Nature Genetics, 2024; Broad Institute
evidence.caveat: Classification accuracy varies by gene and variant type. Clinical validation still ongoing for many variants.

evidence.description: AI base-calling improves sequencing accuracy: DeepVariant, Guppy/Dorado for nanopore
evidence.measured_amplification: 10-50% reduction in sequencing error rates
evidence.source: Poplin et al., 'A universal SNP and small-indel variant caller using deep neural networks', Nature Biotechnology 2018
evidence.caveat: Incremental improvement to existing pipeline, not transformative

counter_evidence.description: Most genomic analysis still requires significant human expertise for clinical interpretation
counter_evidence.source: ACMG Standards and Guidelines for variant interpretation, updated 2024

saturation.notes: Protein structure largely solved; remaining problems (dynamics, interactions) are harder

interactions.weight_justification: Reduced from 3.0 to 2.2. The v5 weight was based primarily on the Google AI co-scientist demonstration at Imperial College, which showed dramatic speedup but is a single institution demonstration. FDA approval timelines have not meaningfully shortened. Insilico Medicine's INS018_055 (AI-discovered drug reaching Phase II) is the strongest real-world evidence, but it is one drug. The weight of 2.2 reflects genuine acceleration in early-stage discovery while acknowledging that clinical trials, regulatory approval, and manufacturing remain bottlenecks that AI has not yet shortened.

evidence.description: Google AI co-scientist replicated years of cancer research in days at Imperial College London
evidence.measured_amplification: ~365x time reduction (years to days) for hypothesis generation
evidence.source: Google Research Blog, February 2025
evidence.caveat: Single demonstration at one institution. Hypothesis generation is early-stage; validation and clinical testing still take years.

evidence.description: Insilico Medicine: INS018_055, first fully AI-discovered drug to reach Phase II clinical trials for IPF
evidence.measured_amplification: Target identification to Phase I in ~30 months vs typical 4-5 years (~2x speedup)
evidence.source: Insilico Medicine, press releases and Nature Biotechnology coverage, 2023-2024
evidence.caveat: Still in clinical trials. Efficacy not yet proven. One drug is not a trend.

evidence.description: Recursion Pharmaceuticals using AI for drug repurposing and phenotypic screening
evidence.measured_amplification: 3-5x increase in screening throughput
evidence.source: Recursion Pharmaceuticals SEC filings and publications, 2024
evidence.caveat: No approved drugs yet from AI-first pipeline

counter_evidence.description: FDA approval timelines have not meaningfully shortened despite widespread AI adoption in pharma
counter_evidence.source: FDA CDER Drug and Biologic Approval Reports, 2024

counter_evidence.description: Eroom's Law: drug discovery has gotten slower and more expensive per approved drug over decades, and AI has not yet reversed this trend
counter_evidence.source: Scannell et al., 'Diagnosing the decline in pharmaceutical R&D efficiency', Nature Reviews Drug Discovery 2012; updated analysis 2024

counter_evidence.description: Multiple AI drug discovery startups (BenevolentAI, Exscientia) have faced clinical failures, suggesting AI speeds target identification but not clinical success rates
counter_evidence.source: Various press reports, 2023-2024

saturation.notes: AI accelerates early-stage discovery but clinical trials, manufacturing, and regulation are rate-limiting steps that AI cannot easily compress

interactions.weight_justification: Reduced from 2.5 to 2.0. Foundation models for robotics (RT-2, Octo) are impressive in demos but deployed in limited settings. Figure 02 and similar humanoids use foundation models but are pre-commercial. The gap between laboratory demonstrations and production deployment is significant. Weight of 2.0 reflects real progress while acknowledging deployment constraints.

evidence.description: RT-2: Vision-Language-Action model enabling robots to follow natural language instructions and generalize to unseen tasks
evidence.measured_amplification: 2-3x improvement in task generalization over prior methods
evidence.source: Brohan et al., 'RT-2: Vision-Language-Action Models Transfer Web Knowledge to Robotic Control', 2023
evidence.caveat: Lab demonstrations, not production deployment. Real-world robustness unproven at scale.

evidence.description: Figure 02 humanoid using OpenAI foundation model for task understanding and execution
evidence.measured_amplification: Qualitative improvement in task understanding; quantitative metrics not publicly available
evidence.source: Figure AI demonstrations and partnership announcements, 2024
evidence.caveat: Pre-commercial demonstrations. No independent benchmarking published.

evidence.description: Tesla Optimus using neural network-based motor control, reportedly performing factory tasks
evidence.measured_amplification: Claimed autonomous operation in Tesla factories for sorting tasks
evidence.source: Tesla AI Day presentations, 2023-2024
evidence.caveat: Limited independent verification. Narrow tasks only.

counter_evidence.description: Most deployed industrial robots still use traditional control systems, not AI/ML
counter_evidence.source: International Federation of Robotics, World Robotics Report 2024

counter_evidence.description: Real-world robot manipulation failure rates remain high for unstructured environments
counter_evidence.source: Multiple robotics competition results (RoboCup, DARPA Robotics Challenge follow-ups)

saturation.notes: Foundation models are transforming robot control but physical world constraints (safety, reliability) limit deployment speed

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. SpaceX uses ML for trajectory optimization and landing guidance, but space launch costs are dominated by propulsion physics and manufacturing scale. AI is a useful tool, not a primary driver.

evidence.description: SpaceX uses ML for trajectory optimization and autonomous landing
evidence.measured_amplification: Modest: improves landing success rate and fuel efficiency by estimated 5-10%
evidence.source: SpaceX engineering talks (various), Lars Blackmore presentations on convex optimization
evidence.caveat: Convex optimization, not deep learning, drives most landing algorithms. ML contribution is secondary.

evidence.description: AI for satellite constellation management (Starlink) and collision avoidance
evidence.measured_amplification: Autonomous collision avoidance for 5,000+ satellites
evidence.source: SpaceX FCC filings and Starlink documentation
evidence.caveat: Operational efficiency, not cost reduction driver

counter_evidence.description: Launch cost reduction is driven by reusability engineering and manufacturing scale, not AI
counter_evidence.source: NASA Office of Inspector General, 'Commercial Space Launch Cost Trends', 2023

saturation.notes: Space launch is physics-dominated. AI plays a supporting role.

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. AI quality control and process optimization are well-documented (10x inspection speed, 2x accuracy). Siemens, Foxconn, and automotive OEMs provide multiple independent confirmations.

evidence.description: AI visual inspection: 10x speed improvement, 2x defect detection accuracy in manufacturing
evidence.measured_amplification: 10x speed, ~2x accuracy for defect detection
evidence.source: Landing AI (Andrew Ng), multiple customer case studies; Siemens industrial AI deployments
evidence.caveat: Applies to visual inspection tasks specifically. Process optimization gains are smaller.

evidence.description: Generative design (Autodesk, Siemens) using AI to optimize part geometry
evidence.measured_amplification: 20-40% weight reduction in designed parts while maintaining strength
evidence.source: Autodesk Generative Design case studies, 2023
evidence.caveat: Applicable to specific part types. Not all manufacturing benefits equally.

counter_evidence.description: Manufacturing productivity growth in developed economies has been stagnant despite AI adoption
counter_evidence.source: US Bureau of Labor Statistics, Manufacturing Productivity data, 2024

saturation.notes: Quality inspection and design optimization are largely one-time improvements

interactions.weight_justification: Reduced from 3.0 to 2.5. GNoME is genuinely transformative for materials discovery (10x increase in known stable materials). However, prediction is not synthesis — most GNoME-predicted materials have not been experimentally verified or manufactured. The gap between computational prediction and real-world deployment is the key bottleneck. Weight of 2.5 still reflects AI as the primary accelerant for materials discovery, acknowledging the prediction-to-production gap.

evidence.description: GNoME (Google DeepMind): discovered 380,000 new stable crystal structures, 10x increase in known materials
evidence.measured_amplification: 10x increase in known stable materials. 736 structures independently verified experimentally.
evidence.source: Merchant et al., 'Scaling deep learning for materials discovery', Nature 2023
evidence.caveat: 380,000 predicted, 736 experimentally verified. Prediction-to-synthesis gap is enormous.

evidence.description: A-Lab (Berkeley): autonomous lab synthesized 41 of 58 AI-predicted materials
evidence.measured_amplification: 71% synthesis success rate for AI-predicted materials; each synthesis in ~17 hours
evidence.source: Szymanski et al., 'An autonomous laboratory for the accelerated synthesis of novel materials', Nature 2023
evidence.caveat: Simple binary and ternary compounds only. Complex materials much harder.

counter_evidence.description: Materials development timelines from discovery to commercial use average 20+ years historically and AI has not yet demonstrably shortened this
counter_evidence.source: National Academies, 'Materials Genome Initiative Strategic Plan', 2021 update

counter_evidence.description: Criticism of GNoME: many predicted materials may not be synthesizable or useful
counter_evidence.source: Leeman et al., 'Challenges for AI in Materials Science', Nature Reviews Materials, 2024

saturation.notes: Discovery is accelerating fast; synthesis and application remain bottlenecks but are also being automated (A-Lab)

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. AI decoders are essential for BCI functionality. Stanford's >95% neural speech accuracy is well-documented. The relationship is strong: BCI is fundamentally an AI decoding problem.

evidence.description: Stanford BCI: AI decoders achieving >95% accuracy in neural speech decoding for paralyzed patients
evidence.measured_amplification: >95% word accuracy, 62 words/minute (near natural speech rate)
evidence.source: Willett et al., 'A high-performance speech neuroprosthesis', Nature 2023
evidence.caveat: Intracortical implants only. Non-invasive BCI accuracy much lower (~70-80%).

evidence.description: Neuralink N1 implant: AI-driven neural decoding enabling cursor control
evidence.measured_amplification: First human patient controlling computer cursor via thought, 2024
evidence.source: Neuralink patient updates, 2024
evidence.caveat: Single patient. Long-term stability and safety data not yet available.

counter_evidence.description: Non-invasive BCI (EEG-based) accuracy remains far below invasive methods despite AI improvements
counter_evidence.source: Various BCI competition results; Wolpaw et al., updated reviews

saturation.notes: AI decoding is necessary but not sufficient — electrode technology and biocompatibility are co-limiting factors

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. AI for quantum error correction is a growing research area. Google used ML for quantum error correction in Willow (2024). Still early-stage with limited demonstrated impact.

evidence.description: Google Willow: used ML techniques for quantum error correction, achieving below-threshold error rates
evidence.measured_amplification: First demonstration of below-threshold quantum error correction at scale
evidence.source: Google Quantum AI, Willow announcement, December 2024
evidence.caveat: ML is one component of the error correction stack, not the sole driver

evidence.description: AI-driven quantum circuit optimization reducing gate counts
evidence.measured_amplification: 10-30% reduction in quantum gate counts for specific algorithms
evidence.source: Various papers from IBM, Google, and academic groups, 2023-2024
evidence.caveat: Incremental optimization, not breakthrough improvement

counter_evidence.description: Quantum computing challenges are fundamentally physics problems (decoherence, error rates) that AI cannot solve directly
counter_evidence.source: Preskill, 'Quantum Computing in the NISQ era and beyond', Quantum 2018 (still relevant)

saturation.notes: Growing but fundamentally limited by physics constraints

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. Scaling laws (Kaplan et al. 2020, Hoffmann et al. 2022) are the most robust empirical relationship in AI: performance scales as a power law of compute. This is the best-documented interaction in the entire model.

evidence.description: Scaling laws: AI model performance scales as a power law with compute, data, and parameters
evidence.measured_amplification: Loss scales as C^(-0.05) to C^(-0.07) with compute C (Kaplan et al.)
evidence.source: Kaplan et al., 'Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models', 2020; Hoffmann et al., 'Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models' (Chinchilla), 2022
evidence.caveat: Power law scaling means diminishing returns: 10x compute yields ~2x benchmark improvement, not 10x.

evidence.description: Training compute for frontier models doubling every 6-10 months (Epoch AI)
evidence.measured_amplification: Each doubling of training compute yields measurable benchmark improvement
evidence.source: Epoch AI, 'Compute Trends Across Three Eras of Machine Learning', 2022 (updated 2024)
evidence.caveat: This rate may slow as costs become prohibitive ($100M+ training runs)

counter_evidence.description: Diminishing returns: scaling laws show power-law, not exponential, returns to compute. Each 10x compute gives ~25-40% benchmark improvement.
counter_evidence.source: Epoch AI analysis of scaling law exponents, 2024

saturation.notes: Power law scaling is robust but inherently shows diminishing returns

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. Anton supercomputer (D.E. Shaw Research) provides 100x speedup in molecular dynamics simulations, directly enabling drug-protein interaction modeling at biologically relevant timescales.

evidence.description: Anton 3 supercomputer: 100x speedup in molecular dynamics, enabling millisecond-scale protein simulations
evidence.measured_amplification: 100x speedup over general-purpose hardware; simulations at biologically relevant timescales
evidence.source: D.E. Shaw Research; Shaw et al., various publications
evidence.caveat: Specialized hardware, not generally available. Simulations still approximate reality.

counter_evidence.description: Most successful drugs are still found through empirical screening, not computational simulation
counter_evidence.source: Mullard, Nature Reviews Drug Discovery annual approvals analysis

saturation.notes: Compute enables longer and larger simulations but molecular dynamics accuracy is fundamentally limited

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. DFT (Density Functional Theory) calculations scale directly with available compute. More compute enables larger unit cells, more complex alloy systems, and higher-throughput screening.

evidence.description: DFT computational throughput scales linearly with available compute; enables high-throughput materials screening
evidence.measured_amplification: Materials Project: 150,000+ computed materials properties enabled by HPC scaling
evidence.source: Materials Project (Berkeley Lab); Jain et al., APL Materials 2013
evidence.caveat: DFT accuracy limited for strongly correlated systems; not all computed properties match experiment

counter_evidence.description: DFT has known systematic errors for certain material classes (transition metal oxides, f-electron systems)
counter_evidence.source: Various computational materials science reviews

saturation.notes: More compute enables more screening but DFT accuracy limits are fundamental

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. Perovskite solar cells are the strongest example — materials science directly enabling next-generation solar technology. Silicon-perovskite tandems reached >33% efficiency (2024), compared to ~26% silicon limit.

evidence.description: Perovskite-silicon tandem solar cells achieving >33% efficiency, surpassing single-junction silicon limit
evidence.measured_amplification: 33.7% efficiency (LONGi, 2024) vs ~26.8% silicon limit — 25% relative improvement
evidence.source: LONGi record, NREL Best Research-Cell Efficiency Chart, 2024
evidence.caveat: Perovskite stability and durability remain challenges for commercial deployment. Lab efficiency =/= field efficiency.

counter_evidence.description: Perovskite solar cells still face significant durability challenges — degradation under moisture and heat
counter_evidence.source: Various reviews in Nature Energy, 2024

saturation.notes: Efficiency approaching thermodynamic limits (Shockley-Queisser for tandems ~45%)

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. LFP (lithium iron phosphate) revolution is the canonical example. Materials innovation (CATL's cell-to-pack, sodium-ion) is the primary driver of battery cost reduction alongside manufacturing scale.

evidence.description: LFP battery revolution: materials innovation drove dramatic cost decline and improved safety
evidence.measured_amplification: LFP enabled ~30% cost reduction vs NMC for energy storage applications; eliminated cobalt dependency
evidence.source: BloombergNEF, CATL financial reports, 2024
evidence.caveat: LFP has lower energy density than NMC — tradeoff between cost and performance

evidence.description: Sodium-ion batteries: materials innovation enabling cobalt-free, lithium-free batteries
evidence.measured_amplification: Sodium-ion reaching $40-60/kWh at cell level, ~50% cheaper than current Li-ion
evidence.source: CATL sodium-ion announcements; various Nature Energy publications
evidence.caveat: Lower energy density, still early-stage manufacturing scale

counter_evidence.description: Most battery cost reduction comes from manufacturing scale (Wright's Law), not new materials
counter_evidence.source: Ziegler & Trancik, 'Re-examining rates of lithium-ion battery technology improvement', Energy & Environmental Science 2021

saturation.notes: New chemistries enable step-function improvements but each new chemistry has diminishing marginal gain

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. Robots ARE manufacturing. Foxconn deployed 100,000+ robots; Tesla is building 'lights-out' factories. This is the most direct causal link in the interaction matrix.

evidence.description: Foxconn deployed 100,000+ robots (Foxbots), reducing headcount by 60,000 at single factory
evidence.measured_amplification: ~40% labor cost reduction in affected production lines
evidence.source: Foxconn financial reports and press releases, 2023-2024
evidence.caveat: Final assembly of electronics still requires significant human labor for fine manipulation

evidence.description: Tesla Gigafactory: among the most automated factories on Earth, using 1,000+ robots
evidence.measured_amplification: Production rate of ~10,000+ vehicles/week with relatively small workforce
evidence.source: Tesla quarterly production reports; Munro & Associates teardown analyses
evidence.caveat: Tesla initially over-automated (2018 Model 3 production hell), suggesting limits to automation

counter_evidence.description: Tesla's 'production hell' (2018) demonstrated that over-automation can slow production — humans remain essential for flexible tasks
counter_evidence.source: Elon Musk acknowledgment: 'Excessive automation at Tesla was a mistake', 2018

saturation.notes: Diminishing returns as the easy-to-automate tasks are automated first

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. Quantum molecular simulation is the 'killer app' for quantum computing. Google Willow (2024) demonstrated quantum advantage for certain chemistry problems. Still mostly theoretical advantage — practical impact years away.

evidence.description: Google Willow demonstrated quantum computation that would take classical computers 10 septillion years
evidence.measured_amplification: Theoretical: exponential speedup for certain molecular simulation problems
evidence.source: Google Quantum AI, Willow paper, Nature 2024
evidence.caveat: Demonstrated on artificial benchmark problem, not a real materials science problem. Practical quantum advantage for materials likely 5-10+ years away.

evidence.description: Quantum simulation of small molecules (H2, LiH) demonstrated on near-term hardware
evidence.measured_amplification: Exact ground-state energies for small molecules (2-20 electrons)
evidence.source: Google, IBM, various academic groups
evidence.caveat: Only for very small molecules. Classically solvable molecules. Not yet beyond classical capability for useful problems.

counter_evidence.description: Practical quantum advantage for materials science requires error-corrected quantum computers with millions of qubits — decades away on current trajectories
counter_evidence.source: Babbush et al., 'Quantum simulation of chemistry with sublinear scaling', 2023

saturation.notes: If/when error-corrected quantum computers arrive, impact on materials science would be transformative. Current impact near zero.

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. GWAS (genome-wide association studies) have identified thousands of drug targets. Genomics-to-drug-target pipeline is well-established and well-documented. Multiple successful examples (PCSK9, BRCA-targeted therapies).

evidence.description: GWAS have identified 10,000+ disease-associated loci, enabling target-based drug discovery
evidence.measured_amplification: Drugs with genetic evidence of target-disease link have 2x higher clinical trial success rate
evidence.source: Nelson et al., 'The support of human genetic evidence for approved drug indications', Nature Genetics 2015; King et al., Drug Discovery Today 2019
evidence.caveat: Most GWAS loci explain small fractions of disease risk; many are not actionable drug targets

evidence.description: PCSK9: genetic discovery led directly to a new drug class (evolocumab/alirocumab) for cholesterol
evidence.measured_amplification: Gene discovery (2003) to FDA approval (2015): 12 years. Now a $2B+ drug class.
evidence.source: Abifadel et al., Nature Genetics 2003; FDA approval records
evidence.caveat: Timeline of 12 years from genetic discovery to drug is still long

counter_evidence.description: Most genetically validated targets still fail in clinical trials due to off-target effects, toxicity, and bioavailability
counter_evidence.source: Cook et al., 'Lessons learned from the fate of AstraZeneca's drug pipeline', Nature Reviews Drug Discovery 2014

saturation.notes: Genetic targets are necessary but not sufficient for successful drugs

interactions.weight_justification: Unchanged. Cheap energy makes large data centres economically viable. Energy is ~30-40% of data centre operating cost. Effect is real but modest — compute cost is dominated by semiconductor manufacturing, not energy.

evidence.description: Energy accounts for 30-40% of data centre operating costs; cheaper energy directly reduces compute cost
evidence.measured_amplification: Each 50% reduction in energy cost yields ~15-20% reduction in total compute cost
evidence.source: Uptime Institute, Data Centre Cost Analysis 2024; Lawrence Berkeley National Lab
evidence.caveat: Data centre siting already optimizes for cheap energy (Oregon, Iceland, Nordic countries)

counter_evidence.description: AI compute demand is growing faster than energy cost decline, creating net energy pressure
counter_evidence.source: IEA, 'Electricity 2024'

saturation.notes: Energy is a declining fraction of compute cost as chip efficiency improves

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. AI is demonstrably useful for grid optimization (5-15% efficiency gains), materials discovery for carbon capture (Microsoft/PNNL paradigm), and climate modeling. Multiple independent evidence sources. Weight of 1.8 reflects genuine utility across environmental applications without overstating impact given that environmental costs are dominated by physical infrastructure and energy input costs.

evidence.description: AI-driven grid optimization reducing transmission losses and enabling higher renewable penetration
evidence.measured_amplification: 5-15% reduction in grid losses; improved renewable integration
evidence.source: NREL, 'AI for Grid Modernization', 2023; Google DeepMind grid optimization deployments
evidence.caveat: Grid is a regulated, slow-moving infrastructure. AI adoption constrained by utility timelines.

evidence.description: AI-accelerated discovery of carbon capture sorbents and catalysts
evidence.measured_amplification: 10-50x screening speedup for candidate materials
evidence.source: Multiple DOE-funded projects; analogous to Microsoft/PNNL battery work
evidence.caveat: Discovery acceleration does not directly reduce carbon capture cost — energy input is the primary cost.

evidence.description: AI climate modeling: improved weather prediction (GraphCast) and climate projection accuracy
evidence.measured_amplification: 10-day weather forecasts competitive with physics-based models at 1000x lower compute cost
evidence.source: Lam et al., 'Learning skillful medium-range global weather forecasting', Science 2023
evidence.caveat: Weather forecasting is not the same as climate intervention

counter_evidence.description: AI's own energy footprint may partially offset environmental benefits
counter_evidence.source: Strubell et al., 'Energy and Policy Considerations for NLP', ACL 2019; updated estimates 2024

saturation.notes: Environmental technology costs dominated by energy and infrastructure, not optimization

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. AI is transforming VR/AR through neural radiance fields (NeRF), AI upscaling (DLSS, FSR), procedural content generation, and real-time environment creation. NVIDIA DLSS alone provides 2-4x frame rate improvement with comparable visual quality. AI avatar generation and real-time translation are enabling new VR applications. Weight of 2.0 reflects AI as a major driver of VR quality-per-dollar improvement.

evidence.description: NVIDIA DLSS (Deep Learning Super Sampling): AI upscaling providing 2-4x frame rate improvement
evidence.measured_amplification: 2-4x frame rate at comparable visual quality; enables higher resolutions on existing hardware
evidence.source: NVIDIA DLSS benchmarks, Digital Foundry analyses, 2023-2024
evidence.caveat: Hardware-specific (NVIDIA only for DLSS; AMD FSR is an alternative with lower quality)

evidence.description: Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) and Gaussian Splatting enabling photorealistic 3D scene reconstruction from photos
evidence.measured_amplification: Minutes to reconstruct photorealistic 3D scenes from 2D photos, vs hours/days for manual modeling
evidence.source: Mildenhall et al., 'NeRF: Neural Radiance Fields', ECCV 2020; Kerbl et al., '3D Gaussian Splatting', SIGGRAPH 2023
evidence.caveat: Static scenes easier than dynamic; real-time rendering of NeRFs still limited

evidence.description: AI procedural content generation for games and VR worlds
evidence.measured_amplification: 10-100x reduction in content creation time for certain asset types
evidence.source: Various game studio reports; NVIDIA Omniverse
evidence.caveat: Quality varies; human art direction still essential

counter_evidence.description: VR headset adoption remains sluggish despite AI improvements — comfort, content, and social factors dominate
counter_evidence.source: IDC VR/AR headset tracking data, 2024

saturation.notes: AI rendering improvements are substantial but VR adoption limited by hardware form factor and content ecosystem

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. AI sensor fusion is arguably the most impactful AI application in consumer technology. Every modern smartphone uses AI for image processing (computational photography). Autonomous vehicles rely entirely on AI sensor fusion. Edge AI inference enables on-device processing that dramatically increases effective sensor capability. Weight of 2.2 reflects the deep integration of AI into sensor value chains.

evidence.description: Computational photography: AI-driven image processing in smartphones providing DSLR-quality from tiny sensors
evidence.measured_amplification: 10-100x effective improvement in image quality from same hardware sensor
evidence.source: Google Pixel computational photography papers; Apple Camera pipeline documentation
evidence.caveat: AI is processing, not sensing — fundamental sensor physics still limit raw data quality

evidence.description: Autonomous vehicle sensor fusion: combining lidar, radar, cameras via AI for superhuman perception
evidence.measured_amplification: Waymo: AI sensor fusion achieving lower accident rate than human drivers per million miles
evidence.source: Waymo Safety Report 2024; NHTSA data
evidence.caveat: Limited operational domains (geofenced cities). Edge cases remain challenging.

evidence.description: Edge AI chips (Qualcomm, Apple Neural Engine) enabling on-device ML inference for sensors
evidence.measured_amplification: 100x improvement in edge AI inference TOPS/watt over 5 years
evidence.source: Qualcomm, Apple chip announcements; MLPerf edge benchmarks
evidence.caveat: Edge AI improves the processing, not the physical sensor itself

counter_evidence.description: Physical sensor improvements (pixel size, sensitivity) follow semiconductor scaling, not AI scaling
counter_evidence.source: Sony semiconductor sensor roadmaps

saturation.notes: AI dramatically improves what can be extracted from sensor data but physical sensing limits remain

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. This is one of the highest-weight new interactions because the relationship is direct and well-documented. Desalination cost is dominated by energy input (~50% of total cost). Carbon capture cost is dominated by energy input (~70% of total cost). Cheap energy directly and measurably reduces environmental remediation costs. Every halving of energy cost roughly halves desalination cost. Weight of 2.5 reflects the direct, causal, quantified relationship.

evidence.description: Desalination energy cost: ~3-4 kWh/m3 for reverse osmosis. Energy is ~50% of operating cost.
evidence.measured_amplification: Each 50% reduction in energy cost yields ~25% reduction in desalination cost
evidence.source: International Desalination Association; Elimelech & Phillip, Science 2011
evidence.caveat: Membrane replacement and capital costs also significant

evidence.description: Direct Air Capture (DAC) of CO2: energy is 60-70% of operating cost for both solid sorbent and liquid solvent approaches
evidence.measured_amplification: Each 50% reduction in energy cost yields ~30-35% reduction in DAC cost
evidence.source: National Academies, 'Negative Emissions Technologies and Reliable Sequestration', 2019; Climeworks, Carbon Engineering documentation
evidence.caveat: Energy cost is necessary but not sufficient — sorbent degradation and capital costs also matter

evidence.description: Green hydrogen production: electrolysis cost directly proportional to electricity cost
evidence.measured_amplification: At $10/MWh electricity (achievable with cheap solar), green hydrogen reaches ~$1/kg — competitive with fossil
evidence.source: IRENA, 'Green Hydrogen Cost Reduction', 2020; BloombergNEF Hydrogen Economy Outlook
evidence.caveat: Electrolyzer capital costs and capacity factors also matter

counter_evidence.description: Cheap energy could increase total energy consumption (Jevons Paradox), partially offsetting environmental benefits
counter_evidence.source: Various energy economics literature; Sorrell, 'Jevons' Paradox revisited', Energy Policy 2009

saturation.notes: Direct causal link but subject to diminishing returns as energy becomes a smaller fraction of total cost

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. Autonomous operations are critical for space because human presence is extremely expensive. Every Mars mission relies entirely on robotic operations. On-orbit servicing (Northrop Grumman MEV) and autonomous satellite operations reduce costs. Weight of 1.5 reflects the real but bounded impact — space costs are dominated by propulsion physics, not on-orbit operations.

evidence.description: Mars rovers (Perseverance, Curiosity) demonstrate autonomous navigation and sample collection
evidence.measured_amplification: Enables science operations with 4-24 minute communication delay; no alternative to autonomy
evidence.source: NASA JPL Mars rover documentation
evidence.caveat: Mars rovers are very slow and cautious — limited autonomy compared to terrestrial robots

evidence.description: Northrop Grumman MEV: robotic satellite servicing extending satellite life by 5+ years
evidence.measured_amplification: $200M-500M value per satellite life extension
evidence.source: Northrop Grumman Space Logistics documentation
evidence.caveat: Narrow application — geostationary satellite docking only

evidence.description: SpaceX autonomous rocket landing — robotics enabling reusability
evidence.measured_amplification: 10-100x launch cost reduction enabled by autonomous precision landing
evidence.source: SpaceX landing documentation; various analyses of Falcon 9 economics
evidence.caveat: Autonomous landing is more guidance/control than robotics per se

counter_evidence.description: Space cost reduction driven primarily by propulsion engineering and manufacturing scale, not autonomy
counter_evidence.source: NASA OIG cost analyses of commercial launch providers

saturation.notes: Autonomy is enabling but propulsion physics dominates space cost

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. Bioinformatics is fundamentally compute-limited. Genome assembly, variant calling, and multi-omic analysis scale directly with available compute. The drop in sequencing cost has been accompanied by (and partly enabled by) massive compute scaling. UK Biobank (500K genomes) and All of Us (1M+) are only possible because of cloud compute scaling. Weight of 1.8 reflects the strong, documented, direct relationship.

evidence.description: Genome assembly and variant calling compute requirements scale with sequencing throughput
evidence.measured_amplification: BWA/GATK pipeline: ~100 CPU-hours per 30x whole genome. Cloud compute made this affordable at scale.
evidence.source: Broad Institute GATK documentation; Google Genomics / DeepVariant
evidence.caveat: Compute cost is now a small fraction of total sequencing cost (dominated by reagents and instruments)

evidence.description: UK Biobank: 500,000 whole genomes analyzed using massive cloud compute
evidence.measured_amplification: Enabled population-scale genomics that was computationally impossible 10 years prior
evidence.source: UK Biobank, Nature 2022; Halldorsson et al.
evidence.caveat: Data analysis is not the bottleneck for most genomics applications — interpretation is

evidence.description: DeepVariant: GPU-accelerated variant calling achieving higher accuracy than traditional methods
evidence.measured_amplification: 5-10x speedup with improved accuracy
evidence.source: Poplin et al., Nature Biotechnology 2018; Google Health
evidence.caveat: Accuracy improvements incremental for common variants

counter_evidence.description: Sequencing cost decline driven primarily by chemistry innovations (Illumina SBS, nanopore), not compute
counter_evidence.source: NHGRI Genome Sequencing Costs data

saturation.notes: Compute enabling but sequencing chemistry and clinical interpretation are the tighter bottlenecks

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. Energy is a significant but not dominant cost in most manufacturing (typically 5-15% of total cost, but much higher for energy-intensive industries like aluminum smelting, steel, cement). Cheap energy enables reshoring of energy-intensive manufacturing. Weight of 1.2 reflects the real but moderate impact for average manufacturing.

evidence.description: Aluminum smelting: energy is 30-40% of production cost. Cheap energy directly reduces aluminum cost.
evidence.measured_amplification: Each 50% energy cost reduction yields ~15-20% aluminum cost reduction
evidence.source: International Aluminium Institute; CRU Group analysis
evidence.caveat: Only applies to energy-intensive industries, not all manufacturing

evidence.description: Electric arc furnace steel: energy is 20-30% of production cost
evidence.measured_amplification: Cheap renewable energy enables competitive green steel production
evidence.source: World Steel Association; various green steel announcements (SSAB, H2 Green Steel)
evidence.caveat: Capital costs and raw materials also significant

counter_evidence.description: For most consumer goods manufacturing, energy is 5-10% of cost — labor and materials dominate
counter_evidence.source: US Census Bureau, Annual Survey of Manufactures

saturation.notes: Impact varies enormously by industry — high for smelting/cement, low for electronics assembly

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. Cheap, accurate sensors are a critical enabler for robotics. Lidar cost dropped from $75,000 (Velodyne 2012) to $500 (Livox 2024). MEMS IMUs dropped from $100 to $1. Force-torque sensors enabling dexterous manipulation. Without cheap sensors, affordable robots are impossible. Weight of 1.8 reflects sensors as a necessary (though not sufficient) enabler.

evidence.description: Lidar cost collapse: $75,000 (2012) to $500 (2024) enabled by automotive demand and MEMS/solid-state designs
evidence.measured_amplification: 150x cost reduction enabling lidar on consumer vehicles and robots
evidence.source: Velodyne/Ouster/Livox pricing; Yole Développement lidar market reports
evidence.caveat: Cheap lidar still less capable than expensive units; range and resolution tradeoffs

evidence.description: MEMS IMU cost decline from $100+ to $1 enabling inertial navigation in every robot and drone
evidence.measured_amplification: 100x cost reduction; now a commodity component
evidence.source: InvenSense/TDK, Bosch Sensortec product pricing
evidence.caveat: MEMS IMU accuracy still far below fiber-optic gyroscopes for precision applications

counter_evidence.description: Robot cost is dominated by actuators and control systems, not sensors (sensors are <10% of typical robot BOM)
counter_evidence.source: Various robot teardown analyses

saturation.notes: Sensors are an enabling but minority cost factor in robotics

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. VR resolution and frame rate are directly limited by GPU/SoC compute capability. VR requires ~7x more pixels per second than flat displays due to dual rendering, high refresh rate, and wide FOV. The Qualcomm XR2 Gen 2 chip specifically targets VR compute requirements. Weight of 2.2 reflects compute as the primary bottleneck for VR visual quality.

evidence.description: VR rendering requires ~7x more compute than flat screen gaming due to stereo rendering, high refresh rate, low latency
evidence.measured_amplification: Each GPU generation (~50% perf increase) enables meaningful VR resolution/quality jump
evidence.source: NVIDIA, Qualcomm XR documentation; Abrash (Meta) predictions on VR compute requirements
evidence.caveat: Mobile VR (Quest) is SoC-limited; tethered VR benefits from desktop GPU scaling

evidence.description: Quest 3: Qualcomm XR2 Gen 2 enabled significant resolution and passthrough quality improvement over Quest 2
evidence.measured_amplification: ~2x GPU performance enabling higher resolution and better mixed reality passthrough
evidence.source: Meta Quest 3 specifications; Qualcomm XR2 Gen 2 benchmarks
evidence.caveat: Form factor, weight, and battery life also constrain VR adoption

counter_evidence.description: VR adoption limited by content, comfort, and social factors more than raw compute
counter_evidence.source: IDC VR/AR market data; Meta Reality Labs financial losses ($16B+ in 2023)

saturation.notes: Compute is a necessary but not sufficient condition for VR quality; human visual system sets an upper bound (~60 PPD retinal resolution)

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. Battery energy density and cost directly constrain mobile robot operational time and cost. Humanoid robots require ~1-5 kWh batteries. At $108/kWh (2025), battery is ~$100-500 per robot. At $30/kWh, battery cost becomes negligible and energy density improvements extend operation time. Figure, Tesla Optimus, and Unitree all cite battery as a key design constraint.

evidence.description: Humanoid robot operating time directly limited by battery energy density (~250-300 Wh/kg current Li-ion)
evidence.measured_amplification: Each 50% improvement in energy density extends robot operating time by ~50%
evidence.source: Boston Dynamics, Tesla Optimus, Figure AI specifications and presentations
evidence.caveat: Battery is one of many cost/performance factors in robots (actuators, compute are also significant)

evidence.description: Drone flight time directly limited by battery: DJI Mavic series improved from 20 to 45 minutes across generations, driven by battery improvements
evidence.measured_amplification: ~2x flight time improvement over 5 years
evidence.source: DJI product specifications across generations
evidence.caveat: Applies mainly to mobile/untethered robots

counter_evidence.description: Many industrial robots are tethered (grid-powered), making battery cost irrelevant
counter_evidence.source: IFR World Robotics Report — majority of installed robots are fixed industrial arms

saturation.notes: Impact strongest for mobile/humanoid robots; irrelevant for fixed industrial robots

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. Sensor performance is fundamentally materials-limited. Infrared sensors (HgCdTe to type-II superlattices), MEMS (silicon micromachining advances), quantum dot photodetectors, and perovskite-based sensors all demonstrate materials driving sensor capability. Weight of 1.5 reflects the documented but incremental impact.

evidence.description: Quantum dot photodetectors enabling new spectral ranges and sensitivity improvements
evidence.measured_amplification: 10-100x improvement in specific spectral bands (SWIR) compared to traditional InGaAs
evidence.source: Various publications in Nature Photonics; SWIR Vision Systems commercialization
evidence.caveat: Niche applications; mainstream visible-light sensors still silicon-dominated

evidence.description: MEMS fabrication advances enabling smaller, cheaper, more sensitive inertial and pressure sensors
evidence.measured_amplification: 10x sensitivity improvement per decade; 100x cost reduction over 15 years
evidence.source: Bosch Sensortec, STMicroelectronics MEMS product evolution
evidence.caveat: Improvements are incremental, following a steady learning curve

counter_evidence.description: Silicon CMOS image sensors have dominated for 20+ years; materials innovations have not displaced them
counter_evidence.source: Sony semiconductor market leadership data

saturation.notes: Materials enable new sensor modalities but silicon dominance is strong for mainstream applications

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. VR provides a controlled stimulus environment for BCI calibration and training. VR-based rehabilitation with BCI feedback is a growing clinical application. The interaction is real but indirect — VR provides the interface, not the neural technology. Weight of 1.0 reflects this supporting role.

evidence.description: VR-based BCI rehabilitation: VR environments provide controlled stimuli for motor imagery BCI training
evidence.measured_amplification: 2-3x improvement in BCI training speed when combined with VR feedback
evidence.source: Various clinical studies; Bockbrader et al., PM&R 2013
evidence.caveat: Small-scale clinical studies; not yet standard practice

counter_evidence.description: BCI performance is dominated by electrode and decoding technology, not the interface environment
counter_evidence.source: BrainGate consortium publications

saturation.notes: Supporting role only — VR is a user interface, not a neural technology

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. Synthetic biology and engineered organisms for environmental remediation is a growing field. Engineered algae for carbon fixation, modified soil microbiomes for nitrogen fixation, and bioremediation organisms are documented applications. Still mostly research-stage. Weight of 1.2 reflects early-stage but genuine potential.

evidence.description: Engineered algae for enhanced carbon fixation: 2-5x improvement in CO2 uptake over wild-type
evidence.measured_amplification: 2-5x carbon fixation rate improvement in laboratory settings
evidence.source: Various synthetic biology publications; Liang & Lindblad, Energy & Environmental Science
evidence.caveat: Lab results; outdoor scaling faces contamination, predation, and environmental regulation challenges

evidence.description: Metagenomic monitoring: genomic sequencing of environmental samples for biodiversity and pollution monitoring
evidence.measured_amplification: Identifies 10-100x more species/pathogens than traditional methods
evidence.source: Earth BioGenome Project; eDNA monitoring studies
evidence.caveat: Monitoring is not remediation

counter_evidence.description: Release of genetically modified organisms faces extreme regulatory barriers in most jurisdictions
counter_evidence.source: Cartagena Protocol on Biosafety; EU GMO regulations

saturation.notes: Regulatory constraints are the binding limit, not technical capability

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. Cheap batteries enable solar/wind to provide dispatchable power, solving intermittency. This is arguably the most important energy transition interaction: without storage, renewables cannot fully replace fossil fuels. Battery storage LCOE has dropped from $500/MWh (2015) to $60/MWh (2025). At $20/MWh, solar+storage undercuts all fossil generation. Weight of 2.0 reflects this critical enabling role.

evidence.description: Battery storage enabling solar/wind to provide dispatchable (24/7) power
evidence.measured_amplification: Solar+storage now cheaper than new gas peakers in many markets
evidence.source: Lazard LCOE Analysis 2024; BloombergNEF
evidence.caveat: Long-duration storage (seasonal) still much more expensive than short-duration (4-hour)

evidence.description: Grid-scale battery deployments growing exponentially: 30+ GWh installed in 2024 (US alone)
evidence.measured_amplification: Enabling higher renewable penetration without grid instability
evidence.source: EIA, 'Battery Storage in the United States', 2024
evidence.caveat: Current installations cover hours, not days or weeks of storage

counter_evidence.description: Seasonal storage (weeks-months) remains unsolved and batteries are not the answer for it — hydrogen or other approaches needed
counter_evidence.source: Sepulveda et al., 'The role of firm low-carbon electricity resources in deep decarbonization', Joule 2018

saturation.notes: Short-duration storage is being solved; long-duration storage is a different problem

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. Cheap launch enables massive satellite constellations for Earth observation. Planet Labs operates 200+ imaging satellites. Starlink demonstrates mass satellite manufacturing. Cheap launch directly enables more sensors in orbit. Weight of 1.2 reflects a real but bounded effect — most sensor value is terrestrial.

evidence.description: Planet Labs: 200+ Earth observation satellites enabled by cheap small-satellite launch
evidence.measured_amplification: Daily imaging of entire Earth's landmass — impossible without cheap launch
evidence.source: Planet Labs SEC filings and mission documentation
evidence.caveat: Resolution still limited compared to aerial/ground sensors for most applications

counter_evidence.description: Most sensor applications are terrestrial — orbital sensors are a niche market
counter_evidence.source: Various sensor market reports

saturation.notes: Impact limited to Earth observation and communications — niche relative to total sensor market

interactions.weight_justification: NEW in v6. Cheap environmental sensors enable monitoring that drives policy and intervention. Air quality monitoring networks, ocean pH sensors, methane leak detection (MethaneSAT) all depend on cheap, ubiquitous sensors. Monitoring is necessary for environmental management but does not directly reduce remediation costs. Weight of 1.0 reflects the enabling but indirect role.

evidence.description: MethaneSAT: satellite-based methane monitoring enabling targeted leak detection and reduction
evidence.measured_amplification: Identifies super-emitters responsible for majority of methane leaks
evidence.source: Environmental Defense Fund, MethaneSAT documentation
evidence.caveat: Monitoring identifies problems but does not solve them directly

evidence.description: Low-cost air quality sensor networks (PurpleAir, etc.) democratizing environmental monitoring
evidence.measured_amplification: 100x more monitoring points than government networks at 1/1000th the cost per point
evidence.source: PurpleAir network data; various citizen science publications
evidence.caveat: Accuracy lower than reference-grade monitors; monitoring =/= remediation

counter_evidence.description: Decades of excellent climate monitoring data have not prevented emissions growth — information does not automatically drive action
counter_evidence.source: IPCC AR6, 'Climate Change 2023: Synthesis Report'

saturation.notes: Monitoring is necessary but not sufficient. The bottleneck is action, not information.

interactions.weight_justification: Cheaper energy reduces propellant production costs (LOX, LH2, methane). SpaceX Starship uses ~3,400 tons of methane per launch. Also enables electric propulsion ground testing and in-situ resource utilization.

evidence.measured_amplification: Propellant is 0.2-2% of launch cost currently, but at scale (1000+ launches/yr) it becomes dominant. Green methane at $1/kg vs $3/kg changes Starship economics
In-situ propellant production on Mars requires ~15 kW for Sabatier process per kg CH4

counter_evidence.point: Propellant is small fraction of launch cost; vehicle reusability matters more

interactions.weight_justification: Solar panel and wind turbine costs are dominated by manufacturing (80%+ for solar modules). Manufacturing learning curves directly drive Wright's Law cost reductions in energy tech. Perovskite solar requires precision manufacturing at scale.

evidence.measured_amplification: Solar module: 80% of cost is manufacturing. LONGi automation reduced cell cost 40% in 3 years (2019-2022)
Wind turbine: Vestas automated blade manufacturing reduced cost per MW by 25% (2018-2023)

counter_evidence.point: Raw material costs (silicon, rare earths) also significant and not manufacturing-dependent

interactions.weight_justification: Battery cost is overwhelmingly manufacturing-driven. Gigafactory scale, dry electrode coating, cell-to-pack design — all are manufacturing innovations. CATL's cost leadership is primarily manufacturing efficiency.

evidence.measured_amplification: Tesla 4680 cell: manufacturing redesign targeting 56% cost reduction per kWh
CATL dry electrode: 30% fewer manufacturing steps → 20% cost reduction

counter_evidence.point: Material chemistry breakthroughs (LFP, sodium-ion) also drive cost reduction independent of manufacturing

interactions.weight_justification: Additive manufacturing (3D printing) revolutionizing rocket engine production. Relativity Space printed 85% of Terran 1. SpaceX prints SuperDraco engines. In-space manufacturing enables orbital construction.

evidence.measured_amplification: Relativity Space: 3D printed rocket reduced part count from 100,000 to 1,000. Manufacturing time from 2 years to 60 days
SpaceX Raptor: 3D printed injector reduced production time 80%, cost 60%

counter_evidence.point: Most launch cost is R&D amortization and operations, not manufacturing per se

interactions.weight_justification: Robot cost is driven by precision manufacturing of actuators, gearboxes, and sensors. Manufacturing scale-up enables mass-produced humanoid robots (Tesla Optimus targeting $20K). 3D printing enables rapid prototyping of custom robots.

evidence.measured_amplification: Tesla Optimus: mass manufacturing targeting $20K unit cost vs $100K+ for current humanoids. 5x cost reduction from manufacturing scale
Harmonic drive gearboxes: Chinese manufacturers (Green Harmonic) reduced cost 60% through manufacturing scale

counter_evidence.point: Software/AI is the bigger bottleneck for robotics than hardware manufacturing

interactions.weight_justification: Space-based solar power R&D (Caltech SSPP, ESA Solaris). Satellite-based grid monitoring and optimization. Reduced launch costs enable orbital energy infrastructure that was previously uneconomical.

evidence.measured_amplification: Caltech SSPP: first space-to-earth wireless power transmission demo (2023). Feasibility requires <$100/kg to orbit
ESA Solaris: feasibility study for GW-scale space solar by 2040s. Requires 100x launch cost reduction

counter_evidence.point: Terrestrial solar is already so cheap that space-based solar may never be economically competitive for Earth use

interactions.weight_justification: Earth observation satellites are critical for climate monitoring, deforestation tracking, ocean health, and environmental enforcement. Cheaper launches enable mega-constellations for real-time environmental monitoring.

evidence.measured_amplification: Planet Labs: 200+ satellites providing daily global imaging. Enabled detection of illegal deforestation reducing Amazon loss 20% in monitored areas
Copernicus/Sentinel: EU satellite data enables methane leak detection. UNEP IMEO tracked 1,500 super-emitters in 2023

counter_evidence.point: Monitoring alone doesn't solve environmental problems — policy and enforcement are separate challenges

interactions.weight_justification: Drug discovery drives genomics R&D investment and demand for sequencing. Pharmacogenomics (drug-gene interactions) is a major driver of sequencing volume. mRNA platform (Moderna/BioNTech) creates demand for rapid genome reading.

evidence.measured_amplification: Pharmacogenomics market $7.3B (2023) → $18.3B (2030), driving sequencing demand 2.5x
COVID mRNA vaccines: drove 10x increase in genomic surveillance sequencing (GISAID submissions)

counter_evidence.point: Sequencing cost reduction is driven more by hardware competition than drug industry demand

interactions.weight_justification: Pharmaceutical neuroscience research informs BCI target identification. Drug companies investing in neuromodulation as alternative delivery. Biocompatible coatings from drug delivery tech improve electrode longevity.

evidence.measured_amplification: Abbott/Medtronic neurostim: pharma-derived biocompatible coatings extend electrode life 3x
Pharma DBS trials identified 15+ new brain targets applicable to BCI research (Nature Neuroscience review 2023)

counter_evidence.point: BCI engineering challenges (electrode density, wireless power) are mostly independent of drug research

interactions.weight_justification: Neural recordings inform neuromorphic AI architectures. Brain-computer interface data provides ground truth for neural network design. Neuralink and similar projects generate neuroscience data that feeds back into AI.

evidence.measured_amplification: Intel Loihi neuromorphic chip directly inspired by BCI electrode recordings. 1000x energy efficiency for spiking neural networks
Brain organoid computing (DishBrain): biological neurons used as compute substrate, informed by BCI electrode technology

counter_evidence.point: Most AI progress is software/hardware scaling, not neuroscience-inspired. The 'brain as model' approach has had limited practical AI impact so far

interactions.weight_justification: Direct neural interfaces are the ultimate VR input device. Haptic feedback, thought-based navigation, sensory augmentation. Even non-invasive EEG-based BCIs are being integrated into VR headsets.

evidence.measured_amplification: Galea (OpenBCI + VR): EEG-integrated VR headset for emotion-responsive environments. Shipped 2023
Meta neural wristband: EMG-based VR input, 10x faster than hand tracking. Acquired CTRL-labs for $500M+

counter_evidence.point: Current BCIs are too low-bandwidth and invasive for consumer VR. Non-invasive options (EEG/EMG) have limited spatial resolution

interactions.weight_justification: Environmental monitoring drives smart grid optimization. Carbon pricing creates demand for clean energy deployment. Environmental remediation tech (DAC) creates baseload demand driving energy innovation.

evidence.measured_amplification: EU ETS carbon price ($80/ton) drove 15% increase in renewable energy investment 2021-2023
DAC at scale (Mt/yr) would require dedicated renewable power plants, driving GW-scale clean energy buildout

counter_evidence.point: Clean energy investment is driven more by economics (LCOE) than environmental regulation

interactions.weight_justification: Environmental constraints drive green materials innovation. Demand for sustainable materials (bio-plastics, green cement, recycled metals) accelerates materials science. Carbon capture requires novel sorbent materials.

evidence.measured_amplification: EU CBAM driving investment in green steel, green cement, green aluminum — materials R&D up 40% since 2020
DAC sorbent research: 500+ novel materials screened 2020-2024, accelerated by need for CO2 capture

counter_evidence.point: Most materials innovation is driven by performance requirements (strength, weight) not environmental regulation

interactions.weight_justification: Quantum computing enables optimization problems intractable for classical AI. Quantum ML (QML) algorithms show quadratic speedups for specific tasks. Google Quantum AI demonstrated quantum advantage for sampling tasks relevant to generative models.

evidence.measured_amplification: Google Willow (2024): quantum error correction milestone, path to 1M+ logical qubits by 2030s
IBM quantum utility experiment (2023): 127-qubit circuit outperformed classical simulation for specific tasks

counter_evidence.point: Quantum advantage for practical ML tasks is unproven. Most QML speedups are theoretical. Classical algorithms keep improving and closing the gap

interactions.weight_justification: Quantum chemistry simulation is the killer app for quantum computing. Molecular simulation at quantum scale enables exact binding energy calculations impossible classically. Could reduce drug discovery from 10 years to months.

evidence.measured_amplification: Google simulated H-chain molecules (2020) — first step toward pharma-relevant molecular simulation
Roche/Cambridge Quantum: quantum molecular simulation partnership targeting 10x faster lead optimization

counter_evidence.point: Fault-tolerant quantum computers with millions of qubits needed for pharma-relevant molecules — still years away

interactions.weight_justification: Quantum computing is a paradigm extension of classical compute. Hybrid quantum-classical architectures. Quantum random number generation. Post-quantum cryptography drives compute architecture changes.

evidence.measured_amplification: Quantum random number generators: 10x faster than classical, commercialized (ID Quantique)
NIST post-quantum cryptography standards (2024) driving hardware changes across all compute

counter_evidence.point: Quantum and classical compute may remain largely complementary rather than quantum replacing classical

interactions.weight_justification: Robotic installation and maintenance of solar and wind. Autonomous drones for wind turbine inspection. Robots enable deployment at scale in remote/hostile locations (offshore wind, desert solar).

evidence.measured_amplification: AES + Terabase: robotic solar installation 3x faster, 50% fewer workers. Targeting $0.01/W installation cost
SkySpecs: autonomous drone inspection reduced wind turbine downtime 30%, O&M costs 25%

counter_evidence.point: Installation labor is <20% of solar LCOE; module cost matters more

interactions.weight_justification: Robotic systems for environmental remediation: ocean cleanup, precision agriculture (reducing pesticide use), autonomous reforestation drones, underwater pipeline inspection preventing leaks.

evidence.measured_amplification: The Ocean Cleanup: autonomous systems collected 200+ tons of plastic from Great Pacific Garbage Patch (2023)
DroneSeed: autonomous reforestation drones plant trees 6x faster than manual crews

counter_evidence.point: Scale of environmental challenges dwarfs current robotic capabilities

interactions.weight_justification: Lab automation is THE driver of genomics throughput. Automated liquid handling, robotic PCR setup, automated sequencing prep. Without lab robots, the cost per genome would be 10-100x higher.

evidence.measured_amplification: Illumina NovaSeq: fully automated library prep → 10x throughput increase, 5x cost reduction per genome
BGI DNBSEQ: automated sample-to-results pipeline reduced sequencing cost to $100/genome (2023)

counter_evidence.point: Sequencing chemistry innovation matters as much as automation

interactions.weight_justification: Bio-manufacturing of materials: spider silk proteins, bio-plastics, mycelium-based materials. Protein engineering enables materials with properties impossible to achieve synthetically.

evidence.measured_amplification: Bolt Threads: engineered microbes produce spider silk protein at industrial scale
Ecovative: mycelium-based packaging replacing styrofoam, grown in 7 days vs petroleum synthesis

counter_evidence.point: Bio-manufactured materials remain niche and expensive compared to petroleum-based alternatives

interactions.weight_justification: More sensor data feeds better AI models. LiDAR, radar, cameras, IoT sensors generate the training data that AI requires. The sensor explosion (100B+ IoT devices by 2030) provides the substrate for AI improvement.

evidence.measured_amplification: Tesla FSD: fleet of 5M+ cars with cameras generating billions of miles of sensor data → 10x improvement in self-driving per year
IoT sensor data: 79 ZB generated in 2025 (IDC), providing training data for industrial AI applications

counter_evidence.point: AI improvement is driven more by algorithmic innovation and compute scaling than by data volume alone (GPT scaling laws)

interactions.weight_justification: Digital twins and VR-assisted design accelerate manufacturing iteration. VR training reduces manufacturing errors. BMW, Boeing, and Siemens use VR extensively for factory design and worker training.

evidence.measured_amplification: BMW: VR factory planning reduced production line setup time 30%, caught 80% of design errors before physical build
Siemens digital twin: reduced time-to-market 20-50% across industrial customers (2023 annual report)

counter_evidence.point: Most manufacturing improvement comes from process engineering and materials, not VR tools

interactions.weight_justification: Grid-scale battery storage enables 100% renewable grids by solving intermittency. Without cheap batteries, renewable energy cannot displace fossil fuels for baseload power, and environmental goals are unachievable.

evidence.measured_amplification: California: 10 GW of battery storage eliminated 60% of natural gas peaker plant usage by 2024
BNEF: battery storage at $50/kWh enables 90%+ renewable grids globally. Current trajectory reaches this by 2030

counter_evidence.point: Long-duration storage (seasonal) requires different technologies than lithium-ion batteries

ai_to_ai_weight.reduction_reason: The v5 weight of 2.5 was the self-identified 'biggest weakness' of the model. After careful analysis, the evidence supports three narrow channels of AI self-improvement (NAS, chip design, coding assistance) but none demonstrate general recursive self-improvement. Key evidence: (1) NAS ~100x speedup but within human-defined search spaces; (2) AlphaChip 10-30% PPA improvement, comparable to one generation of human iteration; (3) Copilot 1.55x on isolated tasks, ~1.3x on real projects. Critical counter-evidence: model collapse (Shumailov et al., Nature 2024) shows AI training on AI output degrades quality. Scaling laws show power-law (diminishing) returns. No documented case of AI recursively improving its own general capability. Weight of 1.8 acknowledges real narrow improvements without assuming general recursive acceleration.
ai_to_ai_weight.sensitivity: This parameter changes 2039 AI improvement median by roughly 2-5x per 0.5 change in weight. At 1.8 vs 2.5, the model produces significantly more conservative (and defensible) AI projections.
ai_to_ai_weight.academic_defense: The weight is derived from documented evidence, not speculation. Each component (NAS: 100x narrow speedup, chip design: 10-30% per generation, coding: 1.3-1.5x) is individually sourced. The composite weight of 1.8 reflects a geometric mean of these effects weighted by breadth of applicability. Counter-evidence (model collapse, diminishing scaling returns) provides an empirical ceiling.

top_5_highest_weight_interactions.justification: Direct causal link: energy is 50-70% of desalination and carbon capture cost. Best-documented quantitative relationship in the matrix.
top_5_highest_weight_interactions.counter_evidence: Jevons Paradox: cheap energy may increase total consumption. Capital costs and non-energy factors also matter.

top_5_highest_weight_interactions.justification: LFP revolution and sodium-ion emergence demonstrate materials innovation as primary driver alongside manufacturing scale.
top_5_highest_weight_interactions.counter_evidence: Wright's Law (manufacturing scale) may explain most of battery cost decline, with materials providing step-function shifts.

top_5_highest_weight_interactions.justification: Scaling laws are the most robust empirical relationship in AI. Power law relationship well-documented across model sizes and architectures.
top_5_highest_weight_interactions.counter_evidence: Diminishing returns: 10x compute yields ~2x benchmark improvement. Training costs becoming prohibitive.

top_5_highest_weight_interactions.justification: AlphaFold solved protein structure prediction. popEVE classified 100M+ variants. Multiple independent confirmations.
top_5_highest_weight_interactions.counter_evidence: Clinical interpretation still requires human expertise. Static structure prediction =/= understanding protein dynamics.

top_5_highest_weight_interactions.justification: GNoME increased known stable materials 10x. A-Lab demonstrated autonomous synthesis. Strongest evidence of AI accelerating a science domain.
top_5_highest_weight_interactions.counter_evidence: Prediction-to-synthesis gap: 380,000 predicted, 736 verified. Materials development timeline not yet shortened.

weight_changes_from_v5.reason: No evidence of general recursive self-improvement. Model collapse counter-evidence. Narrow improvements well-documented but do not support a weight above 2.0.
Google AI co-scientist is a single demonstration. FDA timelines unchanged. Eroom's Law not reversed. Only one AI-discovered drug (INS018_055) in Phase II.
GNoME is transformative for discovery but prediction-to-synthesis gap is enormous. 380,000 predicted vs 736 experimentally verified.
Foundation models impressive in demos but most deployed robots still use traditional control. Lab-to-production gap significant.

new_interactions_added: ai -> environment (1.8): AI grid optimization, carbon capture materials, climate modeling
ai -> vr (2.0): DLSS, NeRF, procedural generation
ai -> sensors (2.2): Computational photography, sensor fusion, edge AI
energy -> environment (2.5): Desalination, carbon capture, green hydrogen all energy-dominated costs
robotics -> space (1.5): Autonomous operations, reusable landing, on-orbit servicing
compute -> genomics (1.8): Bioinformatics scaling, population genomics, DeepVariant
energy -> manufacturing (1.2): Energy-intensive industries benefit directly from cheap energy
sensors -> robotics (1.8): Cheap lidar, MEMS, force-torque sensors enabling affordable robots
compute -> vr (2.2): VR requires 7x more compute than flat displays; GPU scaling directly enables VR quality
batteries -> robotics (1.5): Battery cost/density constrains mobile robot operating time and cost
materials -> sensors (1.5): Quantum dots, MEMS advances, new sensing modalities
vr -> bci (1.0): VR as controlled stimulus for BCI calibration and rehabilitation
genomics -> environment (1.2): Synthetic biology for carbon fixation, bioremediation
batteries -> energy (2.0): Storage solving intermittency, enabling dispatchable renewables
space -> sensors (1.2): Cheap launch enabling satellite sensor constellations
sensors -> environment (1.0): Monitoring networks enabling targeted environmental intervention
